primary_source: Whittaker, 1903. On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics. Math. Annalen 57:333-355. DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.
title: QA Whittaker Rational Direction S^1 FAIL [266] - W3 claimed sup-error bound too tight
_fixture_purpose: FAIL fixture: declares lipschitz_witness with claimed_sup_error_bound = 0.04 for g(theta)=sin(theta), L=1, m=9. The actual sup error (computed independently by validator on a 2048-point uniform test grid in [0, pi/2]) is approximately 0.0522 rad, which exceeds the claimed bound 0.04. Validator must produce a WRD_W3 error: 'declared sup_error_bound=0.04 too strong; actual sup ~ 0.0522'. The theorem bound L*Delta_max^+(9) ~ 0.1993 still holds (the THEOREM is fine); the specific OVERCLAIM in this fixture is rejected.
source_attribution: (Whittaker, 1903) On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics, Math. Annalen 57:333-355, DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.

witnesses.name: overclaim_witness
witnesses._note: 0.04 < 0.0522 actual; validator must catch this overclaim

fail_ledger.rationale: Empirical sup error of |sin(theta)-sin(NN(theta))| over 2048-point grid in [0, pi/2] with D_9^+ samples is ~0.0522 rad, which exceeds the claimed 0.04 bound. Validator independently recomputes and rejects.
